mnemonics: Open Inside, Closed Outside: The interior (Int A) is the largest open set INSIDE A. The closure (Cl A) is the smallest closed set OUTSIDE (containing) A.
keyPoints: The interior of a set A is the union of all open sets contained in A. It consists of all interior points.
The closure of a set A is the intersection of all closed sets containing A. It consists of all points that are either in A or are limit points of A.
A set is open if and only if it is equal to its interior. A set is closed if and only if it is equal to its closure.
commonMistakes: Assuming a point not in A cannot be in its closure. A closure includes all limit points, which may be outside A.
Confusing an open ball around a point with the point being interior. The ball must be entirely contained within the set for the point to be interior.
